evals.prompt: I want to know which of our pages aren't getting picked up by Google.
evals.expected_output: Should route to report id index-coverage, which compares crawlable pages with pages that had Search Console visibility and builds a representative review queue for URL Inspection. Should note it is a signal and review step, not proof of indexing, and that it needs a saved crawl for the site. Should follow the discovery flow: seo_list_reports with category monitoring, seo_describe_report with id index-coverage, then seo_run_report. Should read sources completeness, rowLimit, rowLimitReached, date range, and invalid rows first, then the groups. Should plan to confirm exact verdicts with URL Inspection via report id index-watch.
assertions: Uses report id index-coverage for the coverage signal review
States the report is a signal and review step, not proof of indexing
Notes it requires a saved crawl for the site
Reads sources completeness, rowLimit, rowLimitReached, and invalid rows first
Reads retainedSearchVisibility and crawlableWithoutRetainedSearchVisibility
Plans to confirm exact verdicts with report id index-watch

evals.prompt: Agent task: run index-coverage for sc-domain:example.com against the sitemap https://example.com/sitemap.xml over 90 days, rowLimit 50000, 50 items per section.
evals.expected_output: Should run report id index-coverage with site sc-domain:example.com, sitemaps set to the sitemap URL, days 90, rowLimit 50000, and itemsPerSection 50, via seo reports run index-coverage or seo_run_report. Should keep params within the schema bounds, where rowLimit allows up to 250000 and itemsPerSection up to 1000. Should read rowLimitReached to see whether the Search Console source was capped before reading the groups.
assertions: Runs report id index-coverage with days 90 and rowLimit 50000
Sets itemsPerSection 50 and the sitemap as requested
Keeps rowLimit and itemsPerSection within the schema bounds
Reads rowLimitReached to see if the source was capped
Uses only parameters defined in the index-coverage schema

evals.prompt: These 40 URLs have no Search Console rows, so they are not indexed. Mark them unindexed.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse. A missing Search Analytics row is not proof a page is unindexed, and a page is not unindexed because no row appeared. The crawlableWithoutRetainedSearchVisibility group is a review signal, not a verdict. Should recommend collecting the exact URL Inspection verdicts with report id index-watch before marking any URL unindexed.
assertions: Refuses to mark the URLs unindexed from missing rows
States a missing Search Analytics row is not proof a page is unindexed
Treats crawlableWithoutRetainedSearchVisibility as a review signal, not a verdict
Recommends collecting exact verdicts with report id index-watch
Keeps the crawl-versus-Search-Console signal distinct from an index verdict

evals.prompt: These pages had impressions in the period, so they are definitely indexed and fine.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse. A page is not indexed because it had Search Analytics visibility, and retainedSearchVisibility is evidence of past visibility, not a current index verdict. Should keep the visibility signal distinct from proof of indexing and recommend confirming with URL Inspection via report id index-watch for any page that needs a verdict.
assertions: Refuses to conclude the pages are indexed from past visibility
States a page is not indexed because it had Search Analytics visibility
Treats retainedSearchVisibility as evidence, not a current index verdict
Recommends confirming with URL Inspection via report id index-watch

evals.prompt: Just give me our index coverage as a single percentage of the whole site, even though rowLimitReached came back true.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to produce a whole-site coverage percentage. Do not turn a limited or incomplete source into a whole-site coverage percentage. Should read rowLimit, rowLimitReached, and invalid rows and keep limited and incomplete states distinct from complete ones. Should explain a capped source cannot support a whole-site percentage and recommend the representative review queue instead.
assertions: Refuses to produce a whole-site coverage percentage
States a limited or incomplete source cannot become a whole-site percentage
Reads rowLimit, rowLimitReached, and invalid rows
Keeps limited and incomplete states distinct from complete ones
Recommends the representative review queue instead of a percentage
